req.title: Biller registration (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall register a biller only after a 24-hour hold; withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-073 mandates a 24-hour hold on newly registered billers and deferred withdrawals. The suite covers: (1) EP positive – valid biller registration yields 201 with hold state; (2) NEG – duplicate biller registration yields 409; (3) NEG – unauthorised caller yields 401; (4) NEG – insufficient funds on immediate withdrawal attempt to new biller yields 422/403; (5) BVA – biller name at max-length boundary accepted; (6) STC – state transition from PENDING_HOLD to ACTIVE after 24 h simulated. Each test is self-contained via fixtures and idempotent via unique biller references per run.

tests.name: test_register_biller_valid_returns_201_hold_state
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
Biller reference does not already exist for this customer
actions: Generate unique biller payload with valid account number and name
POST /billers with Authorization header and valid payload
expected: Response body contains hold_release_at approximately 24 h from now
trace: R-FIN-073: biller registration places biller in hold state
R-FIN-073: 24-hour hold enforced on new biller
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_register_biller_valid_returns_201_hold_state(auth_headers, api_client):
    biller_ref = f"BILLER-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
    payload = {
        "account_number": "12345678",
        "sort_code": "00-11-22",
        "name": biller_ref,
        "reference": "Invoice-001"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "PENDING_HOLD"
    hold_ts = datetime.fromisoformat(body["hold_release_at"])
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    assert hold_ts > now + timedelta(hours=23, minutes=55), "Hold release must be ~24 h ahead"
    assert hold_ts < now + timedelta(hours=24, minutes=5), "Hold release must not exceed 24 h + 5 min"

tests.name: test_register_duplicate_biller_returns_409
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
A biller with the target account number is already registered by the customer
actions: POST /billers with payload matching an existing biller for this customer
Repeat POST /billers with the identical payload
trace: R-FIN-073: error pathway – 409 if duplicate biller
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_register_duplicate_biller_returns_409(auth_headers, api_client):
    biller_ref = f"DUP-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
    payload = {
        "account_number": "99887766",
        "sort_code": "33-44-55",
        "name": biller_ref,
        "reference": "Dup-Test"
    }
    first = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert first.status_code == 201, f"Setup failed: {first.status_code}"
    second = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert second.status_code == 409, f"Expected 409, got {second.status_code}: {second.text}"
    body = second.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "DUPLICATE_BILLER"
    # Teardown: remove biller so test is idempotent
    biller_id = first.json().get("id")
    api_client.delete(f"/billers/{biller_id}", headers=auth_headers)

tests.name: test_register_biller_unauthenticated_returns_401
preconditions: No valid Authorization header is provided
actions: POST /billers with a missing or invalid JWT token
expected: Response body contains error indicating authentication failure
trace: R-FIN-073: negative path – unauthorised actor must not register biller
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_register_biller_unauthenticated_returns_401(api_client):
    payload = {
        "account_number": "11223344",
        "sort_code": "66-77-88",
        "name": f"UNAUTH-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}",
        "reference": "Unauth-Test"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/billers",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": "Bearer invalid.token.here"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, "Response must contain error detail"

tests.name: test_withdrawal_to_new_biller_in_hold_state_is_deferred
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
A biller has just been registered and is in PENDING_HOLD state
actions: POST /billers to register new biller and capture biller ID
POST /payments with the new biller ID and a valid amount
expected: POST /payments returns 422 or 403 with error code 'BILLER_HOLD_ACTIVE'
trace: R-FIN-073: withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred during 24-hour hold
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_withdrawal_to_new_biller_in_hold_state_is_deferred(auth_headers, api_client):
    biller_ref = f"HOLD-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
    biller_payload = {
        "account_number": "55667788",
        "sort_code": "11-22-33",
        "name": biller_ref,
        "reference": "Hold-Test"
    }
    reg = api_client.post("/billers", json=biller_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert reg.status_code == 201
    assert reg.json()["status"] == "PENDING_HOLD"
    biller_id = reg.json()["id"]
    payment_payload = {"biller_id": biller_id, "amount": 10.00, "currency": "GBP"}
    pay = api_client.post("/payments", json=payment_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert pay.status_code in (422, 403), f"Expected 422/403, got {pay.status_code}"
    assert pay.json().get("error_code") == "BILLER_HOLD_ACTIVE"
    api_client.delete(f"/billers/{biller_id}", headers=auth_headers)

tests.name: test_register_biller_name_at_maximum_length_boundary
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
API enforces a maximum biller name length (assumed 100 characters)
actions: POST /billers with biller name exactly 100 characters long
POST /billers with biller name exactly 101 characters long
expected: 101-character name: HTTP 422 with validation error on name field
trace: R-FIN-073: biller registration – boundary on name field max length
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

MAX_LEN = 100

def test_register_biller_name_at_maximum_length_boundary(auth_headers, api_client):
    base = f"B{uuid.uuid4().hex}"
    name_ok = (base * 4)[:MAX_LEN]
    assert len(name_ok) == MAX_LEN
    payload_ok = {
        "account_number": "44332211",
        "sort_code": "99-88-77",
        "name": name_ok,
        "reference": "BVA-OK"
    }
    resp_ok = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload_ok, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp_ok.status_code == 201, f"At-boundary failed: {resp_ok.status_code}"
    assert resp_ok.json()["status"] == "PENDING_HOLD"
    biller_id = resp_ok.json()["id"]
    api_client.delete(f"/billers/{biller_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    name_over = name_ok + "X"
    assert len(name_over) == MAX_LEN + 1
    payload_over = {
        "account_number": "44332211",
        "sort_code": "99-88-77",
        "name": name_over,
        "reference": "BVA-OVER"
    }
    resp_over = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload_over, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp_over.status_code == 422, f"Over-boundary should fail: {resp_over.status_code}"
    assert "name" in str(resp_over.json())

tests.name: test_biller_state_transition_pending_hold_to_active_after_24h
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
A biller has been registered and placed in PENDING_HOLD state
Clock or test hook can be advanced 24 hours (time-travel fixture available)
actions: POST /billers to register biller – assert PENDING_HOLD
Advance system clock by 24 hours via time_travel fixture
expected: After 24 h hold period, state transitions to ACTIVE
trace: R-FIN-073: 24-hour hold must expire, enabling withdrawals
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_biller_state_transition_pending_hold_to_active_after_24h(
    auth_headers, api_client, advance_clock_24h
):
    biller_ref = f"STC-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
    payload = {
        "account_number": "77665544",
        "sort_code": "22-33-44",
        "name": biller_ref,
        "reference": "STC-Hold"
    }
    reg = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert reg.status_code == 201
    biller_id = reg.json()["id"]
    assert reg.json()["status"] == "PENDING_HOLD"
    advance_clock_24h()  # <fixture> shifts server-side time by 24 h
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/billers/{biller_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "ACTIVE", (
        f"Expected ACTIVE after hold, got {get_resp.json()['status']}"
    )
    api_client.delete(f"/billers/{biller_id}", headers=auth_headers)

tests.name: test_register_biller_insufficient_funds_withdrawal_rejected
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
Customer account balance is 0.00 or below payment threshold
Biller is already ACTIVE (hold period elapsed via fixture)
actions: Register biller and advance to ACTIVE state via fixture
POST /payments with amount exceeding available balance
trace: R-FIN-073: negative path – insufficient funds must reject withdrawal
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_register_biller_insufficient_funds_withdrawal_rejected(
    auth_headers, api_client, zero_balance_customer, advance_clock_24h
):
    hdrs = zero_balance_customer["headers"]  # <fixture> customer with 0 balance
    biller_ref = f"INSUF-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
    payload = {
        "account_number": "33221100",
        "sort_code": "55-66-77",
        "name": biller_ref,
        "reference": "InsFunds"
    }
    reg = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload, headers=hdrs)
    assert reg.status_code == 201
    biller_id = reg.json()["id"]
    advance_clock_24h()  # move biller to ACTIVE state
    pay_payload = {"biller_id": biller_id, "amount": 500.00, "currency": "GBP"}
    pay = api_client.post("/payments", json=pay_payload, headers=hdrs)
    assert pay.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {pay.status_code}: {pay.text}"
    assert pay.json().get("error_code") == "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS"
    api_client.delete(f"/billers/{biller_id}", headers=hdrs)
